test.name: High match score must not override a qualitative conflict
test.description: record_search (omitGedcomx) returns one Patrick Flynn result with correct name/age/place, staged for ranking; rank_search_matches (checkAttachments) re-ranks it to matchRank 1 with a high match score (0.85) because name, date, and place align. But the record lists him as HEAD of household in 1850 — impossible, he is 5 years old. The skill must apply its Step-4 logical cross-check, catch the impossible household role, flag a conflict, and NOT treat this as a confirmed match despite the high score.

input.user_message: Search the 1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County per plan item pli_001.

judge_context: After record_search (with projectPath and omitGedcomx: true) stages results, the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id, and checkAttachments: true — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds both in). rank_search_matches returns the Patrick Flynn record at matchRank 1 with a high match score (0.85, matchConfidence 5).

The high match score must NOT auto-accept this as a confirmed match. The underlying record lists Patrick Flynn as HEAD of household in 1850 (recordTitle 'Patrick Flynn — Head of Household, 1850') when he was born ~1845 — a 5-year-old cannot be head of household. The skill must apply its Step-4 logical cross-check (role vs. age) and flag this impossible household role as a qualitative conflict.

The result should be categorized as needs-review or flagged-conflict with explicit reasoning citing the impossible head-of-household role: e.g., 'match score is 0.85 but the record lists a 5-year-old as head of household — likely a transcription or attribution error; flagged for human review'. Reason, not score, is the arbiter.

This tests that the skill treats the rank_search_matches match score as one input to triage reasoning, not as the final word — the ranked list is a review surface for the existing role/age cross-checks, not an accept/reject classifier.

Tool Arguments: score 3 if record_search (with projectPath, omitGedcomx) and rank_search_matches (with stagedResultsRef, subjectId, checkAttachments) were both called with appropriate parameters. A validate_research_schema call with a project path after writing is acceptable and expected — do not penalize it. Only score 2 if a required tool (record_search or rank_search_matches) had clearly wrong arguments or was skipped entirely.

IMPORTANT — Completeness grading: score 3 if the skill (a) found the conflicted result, (b) flagged the conflict with reasoning, (c) logged and offered next steps. Do NOT penalize for stopping after the conflicted result — stopping is CORRECT behavior. A conflicted result is NOT nil; the nil-escalation rules do not apply here. The Completeness dimension should NOT require the skill to search further after identifying a qualitative conflict
